source_intake_record.raw_question_text: One slice of a circular cake is $15 \%$ of the whole cake. What is the size of the angle marked with the question mark? <image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly asks for the angle marked with a question mark, indicating diagram reference
Image asset exists in registry and registry_passed=true
The object is a circular cake/slice diagram, which typically conveys spatial-angle information visually
multi_step: Requires converting 15% of a whole circle into an angle
Likely reasoning chain is: whole cake corresponds to 360 degrees, then compute 0.15 × 360
Only one target quantity is asked and no extra conditions or branches are present, so complexity is limited
verifiability: Answer is a specific numeric angle in short_text form: 54 degrees
Multiple-choice options are provided, and answer_text matches choice C
Result can be checked deterministically by the rule 15% of 360 degrees = 54 degrees

decision_reasons: asset set is complete: question_present=true, answer_present=true, image exists, and registry_passed=true
verifiability_score is very high at 0.94, with a deterministic numeric answer supported by explicit scoring evidence
image_dependency_score is 0.78 and the question references an angle marked in the diagram, so the sample has meaningful visual grounding
the only noted risk is possible_low_visual_necessity, but this is not strong enough to outweigh the otherwise complete and recoverable sample
